title: Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs
text:
Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs

Welcome to the website of the Data and Modelling Infrastructure for Living Labs (DMI) Working Group. We are a service working group within the Innovation Center for Agricultural System Transformation (IAT) as part of the Liebniz Center for Agricultural Landscape Research (ZALF). Our mission is to support researchers and partners within the IAT with all things related to data and modelling. We have represented the different ways that we do this in the graphic below:

title: Sarah Johnson
text:
Sarah joined our company in 2018 and has been instrumental in modernizing our technology stack. She holds a Master’s degree in Computer Science from MIT and is a frequent speaker at tech conferences.



Led the migration of our monolithic application to microservices
Reduced infrastructure costs by 40% through cloud optimization
Grew the engineering team from 5 to 50 people
Published research on distributed systems in leading journals





M.S. Computer Science, MIT, 2007
B.S. Computer Engineering, Stanford University, 2005





Email: sarah.johnson@company.com
LinkedIn: linkedin.com/in/sarahjohnson
Twitter: @sarahj_tech

title: Resources and tools
text:
This repo is intended to be a living document for SWG and IAT members to share useful information and resources related to Open Science, programming and Data Visualisation.
Some documents that provide guiding principles:
Check out the Open Science Awesome List

title: Resources and tools
text:
Data visualisation

Fundamentals of Data Visualization
Open Visualisation Academy

title: Resources and tools
text:
R
What they forgot to teach you about R: https://rstats.wtf/

title: Resources and tools
text:
Quarto

Beautiful slides with RevealJS and Quarto

title: Resources and tools
text:
Tools and Software

EU alternatives for software - A directory of software alternatives to popular proprietary software, with a focus on European users and organizations.
gettingstuffdoneai - Directory of Generative AI Tools and Models, categorized by use case and functionality.
Inkscape - Open source vector graphics editor, alternative to Adobe products, useful for creating and editing figures for publications and presentations. Available via the ZALF baramundi client.

title: Resources and tools
text:
Training

Digital Research Academy - a trainer network focused on Open Science, Data Literacy, and Research Software Engineering (DRA’s Zenodo collection).
BERD academy
BERD reproducible research course
NFDI4Earth - Educational Portal
Assured Training -Ensures the safe, legal, and ethical use of sensitive data through targeted training and certification.
KI-campus - Numerous free courses on AI and related topics, including data science, machine learning, and ethical considerations in AI.

title: Modern CSS

Modern CSS
Modern CSS
text:
What is Baseline? A unified way to understand cross-browser feature availability. Features are marked as:

Widely Availabl: Supported in the last 2.5 years of all major browsers
Newly Availab: Available in all major browsers
Limited Availability: Not yet in all browsers




BEST: Fetch https://web-platform-dx.github.io/web-features-explorer/groups/ and find the feature in there, then fetch it’s detail page.
Can I Use: caniuse.com shows Baseline badges at the top of each feature
MDN: Look for the Baseline badge in the browser compatibility table
web.dev: Feature articles include Baseline status

Remember: Always check Baseline status, use @supports for cutting-edge features, and respect user preferences with media queries. Modern CSS is about progressive enhancement and building resilient interfaces that work for everyone.

title: Modern CSS
Modern CSS
text:
CSS Wrapped 2025 - The year’s CSS features
The Coyier CSS Starter - Opinionated modern baseline
Adam Argyle’s CascadiaJS 2025 Deck - (markdownified locally in ./argyle-cacadia-2025-deck.md)
Modern CSS in Real Life - Practical applications

title: Modern CSS
text:
Prioritize Stability:

Recommend Newly Available or Widely Available features for production code.
Use Limited Availability features with progressive enhancement, graceful degredation, or @supports. Or ask the user how they want to handle it.

Use the web platform:

Always prefer standard CSS solutions over JavaScript libraries for layout, animation, and interaction (e.g., use CSS Masonry instead of Masonry.js, Popover API instead of custom tooltip scripts).

Code Style:

Use modern color spaces (oklch) for new palettes.
